behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Unit. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because the behavior text is algorithmic quality at one computation boundary: "heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route"; no required cross-module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or AI output dependence is stated. REJECTION-GATE: higher level was considered and rejected with quoted text.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly with fixed coordinate sets (including edge distributions up to 20 stops) and compare route length against a precomputed optimal baseline dataset; assert ratio <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Unit. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because the behavior text defines a deterministic formula boundary: "computed correctly from the distance ... and the configured average speed". No evidence that failure only appears at module boundaries. No OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Test the estimator function with synthetic leg distances and road-type speed configs; verify per-leg duration and accumulated window math, including rounding and mixed road types.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Unit. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because this is a single validation rule: "rejects ... exceeds ... returning the excess amount". The failure boundary is arithmetic/logic in one validator, not infrastructure or multi-module orchestration. No real OS/network/subprocess/AI needed.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the shift validator with total-estimated-time and remaining-shift-hours values around the boundary (equal, just over, far over); assert rejection flag and exact excess-time output.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Integration. Runner-up: System. Rejected System because the behavior can be reproduced with in-process wiring: geocoder client stub returns "no results" for one address, then verify service-level handoff to optimizer and skipped-address reporting. Quoted behavior evidence for module interaction: "skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped." INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? No. (b) real AI/ML output required? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-governed orchestration? No.
behaviors.test_description: Run a service-level test with real local modules (input parsing, skip/filter logic, optimizer, report builder) and a fake geocoding adapter that returns empty results for one specific address; assert skipped list includes that address and route excludes it while remaining stops are optimized.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Top candidate: Integration. Runner-up: Unit. Rejected Unit because the behavior spans at least deduplication + downstream routing outcome: "consolidated into a single stop ... not visited twice"; a pure dedupe unit test could pass while service handoff still causes duplicate visits. REJECTION-GATE for higher levels: rejected System since no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess; rejected Agentic/Workflow since no AI/ML dependency or agent pipeline is described. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No. (b) No. (c) No. (d) No.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a service integration test with duplicate-address inputs containing distinct delivery items; assert output route has one stop for that address, combined item payload is preserved, and visit count for that normalized address is exactly one.
